g.text: Source-available federation framework for multi-tier AI-agent collaboration · the reference framework for GitAI
Reference framework for GitAI · v0.1 DRAFT · target v1.0 after a full doctrine cycle close + community review · gradus.pk

Reader's map below: inheritance · cross-axis posture · work granularity (4 lanes) · stage taxonomies · bus protocol · load-bearing rules · trade-offs · tunables · UX cross-cutting · failure modes · migration · release
§ Inheritance · Master + Two Concrete Axes + One Future-axis Slot
master · parent · one source of truth
+ status grids · memories · provenance law · 4 work lanes (§40)
• Cycle activator: a module Phase 3 dispatch
▸ Federation is OPEN to new axes. Declare 4 settings + 3 tier roles. Inherit everything else.
Example future axes: review/compliance · ops/deploy · AI-training · research · pen-test/security · whatever the federation grows next.
§ Cross-axis Temporal Posture · Charter state is the switch · axes alternate, never concurrent
The federation's life is alternating epochs. Walk → cycle → re-lock → walk → cycle → …
build axis Phase 3 dormant · Polish/Surgical OK
§40 Work Granularity Spectrum · Four Lanes · small wishes shouldn't elongate through full ceremony
All four lanes coexist within the existing axes. Lane choice is a triage decision at Mentor-1. Load-bearing rules (§§6-§9) apply uniformly; only ceremony level differs.
modal layout · dropdown filter · small feature
Hosted on: build axis · runs alongside Phase 3
Hosted on: build axis · founder-direct triage
§41 Stage Taxonomies per Axis · the lifecycle vocabulary used by status grids, inbox naming, ratification gates
slim Mentor-2 / Doer executes bounded change
smoke pass + UX Critic + lightweight polish tag
Tier may be in any state at any moment:
Shown in Tier 2/3 status grids next to
outgoing END cert + END Status Grid
§ Bus Protocol (universal) + Load-bearing Rules (inherited from master, apply to ALL axes + lanes)
§8 Bus Protocol — universal across all tiers + axes + lanes
Sender writes file into recipient's inbox; commits + pushes; pings founder. Recipient pulls + reads its own inbox.
Mentor-1↔Mentor-2↔Doer uses the SAME mechanism on EVERY axis, EVERY lane (Doctrine Cycle / Phase 3 / Polish / Surgical).
Founder's clipboard carries ZERO substantive content. Single-live-writer + fetch-before-push prevents clobber.
Inbox files KEPT for audit trail. Marker discipline + tag-gated ingestion (§12).
§§6-9 Load-bearing rules — apply ACROSS axes, lanes, presets
founder = de facto Chief UX Officer.
Each tier writes ONLY own folder
• "current WIP" = as of last tagged return
State on disk + at origin BEFORE
discussed with founder. NOTHING in chat.
firewall leak · HYBRID DELEGATED · AI memory
§30 Trade-offs design space + §34 Invariants · Toolings · Specialised Agents (enrichment surfaces; intelligence ↔ cost)
§30 Trade-offs — 5 axes in tension (no combination optimizes all)
Reference impl biases toward: intelligence + low-risk (LAYGO · pure RELAY · fresh-per-slice · xhigh · strict provenance)
7 named operating presets in §35 (Conservative / Balanced / Throughput / Cost-optimized / Risk-averse / Research / Bootstrap) + 3 anti-presets
LAYGO (default) → Pipelined → Parallel-independent → Parallel-doer · trade-off matrix in §31.5
§32 Context patterns: mentor lifecycle · doer scope · cross-tier visibility · memory rate · digest verbosity
§34 Invariants + Toolings + Specialised Agents (intelligence ↔ cost; compound surface)
Project rules holding across all axes.
AI roles tuned for specific work types.
§24 Tunable Parameters · 5 categories · example defaults shown · full matrix in §24 of manifesto
§38 UX excellence (cross-cutting · 4 owners · 5 seams) + §22 Failure modes (5 classes + recovery)
§38 UX excellence — cross-cutting concern (not its own axis)
1. UX-substrate Compass (a UX-doctrine doc) → propagates as constraint set
2. UI/UX Doer persona → specialised agent for UI/UX-laden slices on BOTH axes
3. UX Critic / Click Budget Auditor agent → reviews at doctrine-close + build CP3
4. Founder as UX visionary (§18.4) → the load-bearing 4th role · vision · taste · click budgets
5 seams across axes (where UX is examined):
▸ Doctrine S1-S5 · ▸ Build CP1 (premise) · ▸ Build CP2 (impl) · ▸ Build CP3 (audit) · ▸ Cycle close (founder demo)
Typical stakes (why it can be a main focus):
non-technical users · operators at scale (click count matters) · multi-market literacy · multi-archetype mental models
§22 Failure modes — 5 classes, each with recovery
Recovery: fresh session pulls reviewer-state repo; resume from
last clean checkpoint. Loses ≤1 turn IF flush=push honored.
Recovery: Mentor-1 END cert + push; incoming pulls, T0, Layer-B,
countersign. Sub-tier dispatches continue under new Mentor-1.
Recovery: §6.2 state-tracking rule. Tag notes as "as of date,"
never "live." Forensic descent to verify before action.
Recovery: pure RELAY (sub-agent isolation isn't enough;
returns leak via parent context). Tested, rejected, reverted.
Recovery: provenance law (§23) — verify against substrate cycle-tip
Recovery: Doer escalates BRIEF-INCOMPLETE; forbidden to improvise.
All defended by the trust anchor (§9.1): state on disk + at origin BEFORE discussed.
If state isn't on disk, it doesn't exist.
§39 Migration in 3 phases (existing federation → CompassAlpha) + Release Plan
Author master in proposals/ · confined · running cycle undisturbed
Templates thin out · reference master by §
Release plan: v0.1 DRAFT now (proposals/) → cycle-close = Phase 2 → v1.0 stable after a full cycle close + community review · License: Apache 2.0
